name: Basic Sourdough Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix the sourdough starter with warm water until smooth.
2. Cover and let stand in a warm, draft-free place for 8 hours.
3. Gradually add 3-3/4 cups to 4-1/4 cups flour, sugar, salt, baking soda, and oil to the mixture.
4. Stir until smooth and all flour is completely absorbed. (The dough should be firm enough to gather into a ball.)
5. Turn dough onto a heavily floured surface; knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
6. Place in greased bowl; turn greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1-1/2 hours.
7. Dough is ready if an indentation remains when touched.
8. Punch down dough. Divide into halves. Shape each half into a round, slightly flat loaf.
9. Do not tear the dough by pulling.
10. Place loaves in opposite corners of a greased cookie sheet.
11. Make three 1/4-inch deep slashes in each loaf.
12. Let rise until doubled, about 45 minutes.
13. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
14. Brush loaves with cold water.
15. Place the cookie sheet in the center of the oven; it should not touch the sides of the oven.
16. Bake, brushing occasionally with water, until loaves sound hollow when tapped, about 50 minutes.
17. Remove from cookie sheets and cool on wire racks.
Be sure to let dough rise completely both times – it might take longer than the estimated times given.
